## [00:00 - 03:01]

I'm John Loomer Understanding this changes everything Let's go Your entire approach to meta-advertisers so your strategies when you customize and when you let the algorithm take over And when you trust results and when you're skeptical it all Changes when you understand this fundamental fact What's that fact the ads algorithm will do exactly what it says it's going to do that might not mean great results But it's literal So if you're working in a cloud of uncertainty about what to do and if you're constantly searching out the opinions of others This will change your outlook. How about an example? common strategy traffic campaigns you set a performance goal to Maximize link clicks when you do that the algorithms goal is now to get you as many link clicks as Possible within your budget the algorithm understand does not care about who these people are It doesn't matter whether they're current customers or might eventually buy from you It doesn't matter whether they're men or women old or young It doesn't matter whether those clicks lead to other actions or if they immediately return to Facebook It's literal. It is only going to find you clicks So this is why when you optimize for link clicks you can run into all kinds of problems You can end up spending on men even though your target customer is women why if men click the algorithm Thanks, that's what you want. Well people over 65 were giving you cheap clicks And that's what you want and this is why a high percentage of your budget might go to audience network This is the placement where we'll often find Accidental clicks bots before they're detected and most importantly cheap clicks and guess what? That's what you wanted once you know that the algorithm is literal You understand that this is gonna happen and you understand why it might happen and it's why you might make some changes It's why you might switch to original audiences to have more control over age and gender in this type of situation Because you want to eliminate those cheap clicks from irrelevant people It's why you might remove audience network in this particular situation Because you don't want those cheap clicks you want quality clicks

## [03:01 - 06:03]

And it's why you might consider remarketing to narrow your audience in this type of situation and add some guardrails Because you don't want the algorithm to chase cheap low quality clicks Here's where there's some confusion Oftentimes I'll hear people say oh, I always remove audience network No, it doesn't mean that these things are always a risk when you understand that the algorithm is literal You know why you don't always need to make these Customizations here's another example you set a performance goal to maximize conversions where the conversion event is a purchase the algorithms goal in that case is to get you as many Purchases as possible within your budget. So you and the algorithm there are on the same page It's going to prioritize Remarketing and search out new customers who are likely to buy It's not going to be influenced by cheap clicks because its goal is to find you purchases If men click and don't buy but women click and buy the algorithm will learn from that It will learn from which age groups buy and which click and leave So this is why when optimizing for purchases you don't need to make Customizations in most cases you can target broadly or use advanced plus audience You can include all ages and genders You can use advanced plus placements and leave audience network on so here's what I want you to do today I want you to embrace this simple fact the fact that the algorithm is literal is Foundational and it will completely change your approach and the examples go on and on for your approach to things like your ad sets attribution settings ad creative and results when you understand this you have a consistent and simplified Approach rather than flailing with new theories based on some crazy strategy You heard on the internet and you can focus on the things that matter most and make the biggest impact I Create a ton of free content and I admit it look if you run an agency You're busy You can't keep up with everything.
